title: Service Level Agreement (SLA)
description: A technical IT vendor agreement detailing service availability (uptime), response times, server hosting maintenance, and downtime credits.
whatsCovered: Definition of service scope and cloud hosting availability
Support ticket priority response times (Severity 1 to Severity 3)
Service credit calculations for uptime violations
Scheduled server maintenance windows and notice times
whenToUse: Use this SLA when you are a SaaS or hosting vendor committing to specific performance benchmarks, or a client securing uptime guarantees from an IT provider.

fields.label: Vendor Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 102 hosting Circle, San Jose, CA 95112

clauses.heading: SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This Service Level Agreement (the "SLA") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {client_name}, located at {client_address} ("Client"), and {vendor_name}, located at {vendor_address} ("Vendor"). This SLA governs the hosting and cloud services provided by Vendor.

clauses.heading: 1. Uptime Commitment
clauses.body: Vendor guarantees that the cloud hosting services shall achieve a monthly uptime availability of at least {uptime_percentage}% (excluding scheduled maintenance). Availability is calculated on a monthly billing cycle basis.

clauses.heading: 2. Help Desk Response Times
clauses.body: Vendor shall maintain a technical support help desk. For critical (Severity 1) hosting errors that block core application operations, Vendor shall respond to Client's support ticket within {support_response} hours.

clauses.heading: 3. Service Credits
clauses.body: If Vendor fails to meet the {uptime_percentage}% uptime guarantee in any given month, Client shall be entitled to request a service credit equal to 10% of that month's subscription fee, applied to the next billing invoice.

clauses.heading: 4. Scheduled Maintenance Notice
clauses.body: Vendor shall provide Client with at least 48 hours' written notice prior to conducting any scheduled hardware upgrades or database maintenance that might result in service downtime.

clauses.heading: 5. Governing Law
clauses.body: This SLA shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is excluded from uptime calculations?
faq.answer: Uptime metrics exclude scheduled server maintenance (with 48h notice), client-caused network blockages, and Force Majeure (natural disasters) events.

faq.question: How do I claim service credits?
faq.answer: You must submit a credit request ticket to the vendor helpdesk within 30 days after the end of the month in which the uptime violation occurred.
